The Knowledge Graph provides the semantic backbone: entity understanding, master data relationships, and business context that ground every agent interaction in meaning rather than guesswork.
Key Takeaways:
  • Understand the architecture of Joule Work as the unified engagement layer
  • Learn how the Knowledge Graph provides semantic and knowledge context: the difference between data and meaning for AI reasoning
  • Identify what your systems need to expose (APIs, events, semantic metadata) for Joule Work to operate effectively
Register for Session 2

 Session 3 | 8 September 2026

The Architect's Playbook for Building Your Own SAP Agents
Speakers: Jason Luo, Venkateshwaran Viswanathan, Gaurish Dessai
The SAP Business AI Platform provides a unified environment for building AI agents grounded in business context, but where do you start? What decisions define whether your agent works in a demo or in production? This session is a hands-on walkthrough of building an agent from scratch using the SAP stack: from Joule Studio and the SAP Integration Suite to the AI Core runtime. You will see the full development lifecycle: defining agent capabilities, grounding them in business data and process context, testing behaviour, and preparing for governed deployment.
Key Takeaways:
  • Walk through the end-to-end agent development lifecycle on the SAP Business AI Platform
  • Understand decision points: when to use deterministic applications vs. agents vs. agentic workflows vs. Knowledge Graph queries
Register for the Series

 Session 4 | 22 September 2026

An Architect's Guide to AI Agent Lifecycle Management and the SAP Family of Models
Speakers: Laurenz Vogt, Mock Seng Chong
As AI agents proliferate across enterprise landscapes, organisations face a defining architectural challenge: how do you discover, govern, secure, and measure the business impact of hundreds of autonomous agents? This session explores SAP's end-to-end approach to AI agent lifecycle management through the SAP AI Agent Hub (built on LeanIX), covering discovery and registration through governance, observability, and decommissioning. It also takes a deeper look at the SAP Family of Models, including RPT 1.5 which delivers 3.5x higher prediction quality than generic LLMs because it is pre-trained with business context.
Key Takeaways:
  • Understand the full agent lifecycle (discover, register, govern, observe, decommission) and how it maps to existing EA governance frameworks
  • Learn how the SAP AI Agent Hub on LeanIX connects agents to business capabilities, value streams, and organisational context
  • Learn what makes SAP's purpose-built models structurally different from generic LLMs
  • Identify the governance operating model needed before scaling from tens to hundreds of agents
Register for the Series

Session 5 | 29 September 2026

 

The AI North Star Architecture and AI Golden Path: From Reference to Reality
Speakers: PVN Pavankumar, Paul Craigie
Every architect navigating the Autonomous Enterprise asks the same question: what does the target architecture actually look like, and how do I get there from where I am today? The AI-Native North Star Architecture defines how SAP envisions the convergence of AI, data, and applications across the full stack. The AI Golden Path complements this with a guided journey from idea to production-ready application, including detailed decision trees for agents vs. deterministic applications vs. Knowledge Graph queries vs. HANA Vector Engine vs. agentic workflows vs. code-based agents. This session brings both to life with live demonstrations and worked examples.
Key Takeaways:
  • Understand the AI-Native North Star Architecture as the target state
  • Use the AI Golden Path decision framework for consistent build-vs-buy and pattern-selection decisions
  • See live demonstrations of how reference patterns translate into working implementations
  • Learn how to use published assets from the SAP Architecture Center as alignment tools with stakeholders
Register for the Series

 Session 6 | 6 October 2026

Data Foundations for an Autonomous Enterprise: From Fragmented Systems to Trusted Context
Speakers: Niclas Schautkotter, Alan Beven
The difference between isolated AI pilots and sustainable AI at scale lies in the foundational data architecture. Without trusted, connected, contextual data, agents hallucinate, predictions fail, and governance becomes impossible. This session covers how SAP brings data and AI together with a deep dive into SAP Business Data Cloud, including zero-copy data extraction, delta share protocol, BW modernisation, and the evolving role of the Data Architect in an autonomous landscape.
Key Takeaways:
  • Understand why Data Context is the foundational layer of the Context System
  • Learn the architecture of SAP Business Data Cloud and BDC Connect, including zero-copy patterns
  • Understand the evolving role of the Data Architect: from ETL designer to context curator for AI agents
Register for the Series

Session 7 | 13 October 2026

 

The Role of Enterprise Architects in Driving the Autonomous Enterprise
Speakers: Paul Kurchina, Dirk Treusch, Johannes Euler, Alex Aitken
The closing session that brings it all together. When AI agents execute processes, orchestrate across domains, and make decisions grounded in business context, the architect's role shifts from system design to shaping the operating model, governance boundaries, and transformation roadmap. This session explores what the Autonomous Enterprise means for Enterprise Architects: how decision-making changes, what new competencies become essential, and how EAs position themselves as strategic connectors between business ambition and AI-enabled execution. Includes a complete series wrap-up and clear next steps.
Key Takeaways:
  • Understand how the EA role evolves: from system integrator to transformation orchestrator and governance steward
  • Learn new competency areas: agent lifecycle governance, context architecture design, cross-domain orchestration, AI-native operating models
  • Take away a consolidated view of the series: key patterns, architectural decisions, and reusable assets
  • Connect with a community of architects navigating the same transition
Register for the Series

 Who Should Attend

This series is designed for:
  • Enterprise Architects evaluating AI-native operating models and governance frameworks
  • Solution Architects designing agent-based solutions on the SAP stack
  • Data Architects building the contextual data foundations that AI agents depend on
  • Technology Leaders responsible for AI strategy and platform decisions across Asia-Pacific
Whether you are early in your AI journey or already building agents in production, each session provides actionable architectural guidance grounded in real platform capabilities.
 
All sessions run at 11:30 AM IST | 14:00 SGT | 16:00 AEST | 08:00 CEST, designed for Asia-Pacific time zones with accessibility for colleagues in Europe.
Registration is open now and is completely free for everyone!
 
Register for all sessions: url.sap/aewebinar-apac
 
Each session is independent, but the series is designed as a progressive architecture journey. Attending all seven sessions will give you the complete picture. Select the sessions relevant to your role, or join the full series to build a comprehensive understanding of what the Autonomous Enterprise means for your architecture practice.

entry.title: Certified as an SAP Data Architect? Join the practitioner discussion
entry.content:
Congratulations to everyone who has completed or is working toward the new SAP Data Architect certification. Becoming certified is an important milestone. It shows that you are building the knowledge needed to design scalable, governed, AI-ready data ecosystems and connect architecture concepts to business outcomes. 

But certification is also just the beginning. Once you start applying these concepts in real projects, new questions naturally come up: 

  • How do I translate architecture principles into implementation decisions? 
  • How should I approach governance, metadata, ownership, and data quality? 
  • How do I connect SAP Business Data Cloud with existing SAP and non-SAP landscapes? 
  • How do data architecture choices change when AI, advanced analytics, or data products are involved? 
  • What patterns are other practitioners using in the field? 

That is where the Data Professionals group comes in. The Data and Analytics Learning Group is the right place to learn, prepare, and ask questions about learning content. The Data Professionals group is the next step: a practitioner community for discussing real-world architecture, governance, integration, analytics, AI, and SAP Business Data Cloud scenarios with peers, SAP experts, customers, and partners. 

If you have completed the certification, are preparing for it, or are applying the learning journey in your current role, we invite you to join the practitioner discussion here. Bring your project questions, your lessons learned, your architecture challenges, and your practical experience. 

Why don’t you start with one real-world data architecture question you would like to discuss with other practitioners?

entry.title: What does the Reltio Acquisition Mean for Master Data Management at SAP?
entry.content:
Master data is the foundation of every trusted business process, analytical insight, and AI use case. With the introduction of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) and the recent acquisition of Reltio, SAP customers now have a more powerful set of choices for how to govern, unify, and trust their master data. Since the SAP Sapphire announcement, one question keeps surfacing from customers and partners alike: When should we choose SAP Master Data Governance (MDG), when should we choose Reltio, and when do we need both?

And if you’re a long-standing SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) customer - like the enterprises that have invested in customizing SAP MDG to fit their global master data management (MDM) and governance processes - SAP’s Reltio acquisition likely triggered a mix of curiosity and concern:

  • Does Reltio replace SAP MDG?
  • What happens to our years of MDG customization and process integration?
  • Where does Reltio fit in with our data, analytics, and AI landscape?
  • How and when do we get started?

The short answer: your SAP MDG investment is preserved, modernized, and extended. Reltio will be a complementary MDM capability inside SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) with its own set of strengths based on the customer’s desired outcome.

Why Master Data Management (MDM) Matters More Than Ever

SAP’s strategic direction - the Autonomous Enterprise - depends on a strong foundation: the SAP Business AI Platform, which combines deep process context, unified data, and purpose-built models with enterprise governance, so you can build and integrate AI that understands your business, not just your prompts. A core component of that platform is SAP Business Data Cloud, the data foundation that unifies, governs, and delivers AI-ready data across SAP and third-party sources. 

  1. Foundation: Unified Lake Storage
    At the foundation, lake storage unifies SAP and non-SAP data across multi-cloud, hybrid, and on-premises environments. This layer remains essential and enduring.
  2. Intelligent Compute
    Above the foundation, data is processed through intelligent compute, powering data lakehouses, warehouses, machine learning, and data engineering workloads-the areas where most innovation has occurred over the past decade.
  3. The Knowledge Core (What Changes Everything)
    What’s new and truly transformative is above the compute layer. The knowledge core introduces a paradigm where data is no longer merely stored and processed but understood in the context of how the business operates.

This is why metadata is foundational to the entire system. And not just technical metadata like schemas or pipelines, but active, business-aware metadata that carries meaningful context, including definitions of metrics and KPIs, relationships between data entities, business processes and rules, lineage, usage, and quality signals.

This metadata acts as connective tissue, embedding business context directly into the data so it can be consistently understood and acted upon across the entire organization.

  1. Agents and Applications
    This contextual understanding then powers the top layer: agents and applications capable of self-learning and making autonomous decisions.

The true power of this architecture lies in how value compounds with each layer:

  • Intelligent compute delivers speed and scale
  • The knowledge core provides trust and business understanding
  • Agents deliver autonomous action grounded in that understanding

Together, these layers form a continuous cycle rather than a linear pipeline. High quality data informs context, context shapes decisions, and those decisions generate new data that continuously refines and improves the system.

Within this architecture, master data management is being introduced as a part of SAP BDC’s Knowledge Core, delivered through SAP MDG and Reltio as complementary components - both producing unified data products (what MDM experts have historically called the “golden record” or, in Reltio’s terminology, the “trusted record”).

SAP MDG: Central Governance at the Source

SAP Master Data Governance is about getting master data right from the start. It enforces governance within business processes at the point of creation, typically SAP Cloud ERP. Key strengths include:

  • Create trusted, governed master data at the source 
  • Enforce compliance and standards in every process 
  • Distribute directly to applications and to SAP BDC
  • Provide clean master data for all downstream consumption 
  • Ensure full transparency and auditability of changes 

The value promise: Data quality governed at the point of entry.

SAP MDG is now available as a commercial offering inside SAP BDC and delivered as SAP MDG private cloud, extra stack. Bi-directional integration between SAP MDG and the SAP S/4HANA remains a cornerstone of its value.

Reltio: Data Unification Across Any Source

Where SAP MDG governs at the source, Reltio unifies master data across systems including non-SAP applications and unstructured data through cloud-native, real-time integration. Reltio brings two complementary capabilities into the BDC portfolio:

  1. Data Unification
  • Unify master data across all systems into one view 
  • Resolve duplicates and create a consistent, trusted record 
  • Enable 360° visibility across domains (customers, suppliers, products), including unstructured sources 
  • Deliver unified data for any application or data product, with real-time operational access 

The value promise: High-quality trusted records for analytics and AI.

  1. Data Quality & Profiling
  • Assess and monitor data quality in disparate sources 
  • Make data issues visible across domains and systems 
  • Prioritize remediation based on business impact 
  • Continuously improve data quality at scale 

The value promise: Trusted data through ongoing monitoring and remediation.

Reltio is the unique provider enabling the SAP MDM portfolio in BDC to produce unified data products, reflecting a single source of truth.

Better Together: A Complementary Architecture

The vision SAP is executing toward is a single layer of master data management inside SAP BDC, where customers consume MDM as capabilities - spending SAP BDC capacity units - without needing to worry about which underlying component (SAP MDG or Reltio) is doing the work.

Here’s how the complementary architecture works today:

  • Producers (bottom): SAP Cloud ERP, SAP Ariba, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Commerce Cloud, and non-SAP applications feed data into BDC. 
  • Master Data Management in BDC (middle): SAP MDG (central governance at the point of entry in SAP Cloud ERP) and Reltio (cloud-native unification from any source) sit side by side, both producing high-quality master data that contribute towards trusted, unified data products.
  • Consumers (top): Joule agents, SAP CAP applications, SAP Analytics via SAP Datasphere, and SAP & non-SAP business applications consuming data in real time - including AI workloads grounded in trusted data. 

It’s a Choice of Data Strategy

The choice between SAP MDG and Reltio isn’t really an either/or - it’s an architecture driven decision based on an organization’s governance model, source landscape, and desired outcomes:

  • Use SAP MDG when governance and process control at the SAP application source is the priority. 
  • Use Reltio when unification, 360° views, and cross-system data quality - including non-SAP sources - are the priority. 
  • Use both when the desire is governed creation and enterprise-wide unification, which is increasingly the norm for AI-ready enterprises. 

For heavy SAP MDG adopters:

  • Your investment in SAP MDG is preserved and expanded as SAP MDG becomes a SAP BDC component. 
  • Reltio does not replace SAP MDG. Instead, it extends your MDM footprint beyond the SAP application landscape into non-SAP systems, unstructured data, and 360° domain views. 
  • You can begin adopting Reltio for use cases such as cloud-native unification, cross-system data quality, and 360° profile capabilities needed for a modern, AI-ready enterprise.

Final Thoughts

SAP’s long-term vision is clear: one coherent MDM layer inside SAP BDC, delivering high-quality unified data products that fuel analytics, operations, and AI.

For existing SAP MDG customers, the Reltio acquisition isn’t a disruption - it’s an expansion of what SAP MDM can do for you. SAP MDG today is the standard for governing master data at the SAP application source. Reltio brings the cloud-native unification, cross-system data quality, and 360° profile capabilities needed for a modern, AI-ready enterprise.

The most successful MDM customers will treat this not as a product decision, but as an architecture decision - using each solution where it plays to its strengths, and consuming both as capabilities inside SAP Business Data Cloud.
